ddls:
CREATE TABLE "seismic_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each seismic event */,
  "rup_mag" REAL /* Rupture magnitude of the event */,
  "rup_rake" REAL /* Rake angle of the event */,
  "dist_rrup" REAL /* Distance to the rupture (Rrup) in kilometers */,
  "site_vs30" REAL /* Average shear wave velocity in the top 30 meters (Vs30) in meters per second */,
  "result_type" TEXT /* Type of result (e.g., MEAN, STD) */,
  "damping" REAL /* Damping level in percent */,
  "ia" REAL /* Intensity measure (IA) value */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the event */,
  "event_location" TEXT /* Geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude) of the event */,
  "depth" REAL /* Depth of the event in kilometers */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of seismic event (e.g., earthquake, aftershock) */,
  "source_mechanism" TEXT /* Source mechanism of the event (e.g., strike-slip, reverse) */,
  "magnitude_type" TEXT /* Type of magnitude (e.g., Mw, Ms) */,
  "event_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "seismic_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each seismic data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source (e.g., USGS, IRIS) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the source (e.g., Seismograph Network, Historical Catalog) */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL to the source's website or data repository */,
  "source_quality" TEXT /* Quality rating of the data source (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "source_reliability" TEXT /* Reliability rating of the data source (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "source_license" TEXT /* License under which the data is provided */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_sources" (
  "event_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event-source relationship */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the seismic event */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the seismic data source */,
  "date_collected" TEXT /* Date the data was collected */,
  "source_data_quality" TEXT /* Quality rating of the data provided by the source for this event */,
  "source_data_reliability" TEXT /* Reliability rating of the data provided by the source for this event */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data (e.g., seismograph, satellite) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_sources_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES seismic_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_sources_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES seismic_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "site_conditions" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the site */,
  "site_location" TEXT /* Location of the site */,
  "site_description" TEXT /* Description of the site */,
  "site_vs30" REAL /* Average shear wave velocity in the top 30 meters (Vs30) in meters per second */,
  "site_coordinates" TEXT /* Geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude) of the site */,
  "site_elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the site in meters */,
  "site_soil_type" TEXT /* Type of soil at the site (e.g., clay, sand) */,
  "site_geology" TEXT /* Geologic description of the site */,
  "site_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_sites" (
  "event_site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event-site relationship */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the seismic event */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site */,
  "distance_to_site" REAL /* Distance from the event's epicenter to the site in kilometers */,
  "site_data_quality" TEXT /* Quality rating of the site data */,
  "site_data_reliability" TEXT /* Reliability rating of the site data */,
  "data_collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the site data (e.g., field survey, remote sensing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_sites_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES seismic_events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_sites_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES site_conditions ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "result_types" (
  "result_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result type */,
  "result_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the result type (e.g., MEAN, STD) */,
  "result_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the result type */,
  "result_type_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the result type (e.g., g, m/s²) */,
  "result_type_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the result type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "damping_levels" (
  "damping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each damping level */,
  "damping_value" REAL /* Value of the damping level in percent */,
  "damping_description" TEXT /* Description of the damping level */,
  "damping_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the damping level (e.g., %) */,
  "damping_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the damping level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("damping_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the seismic event */,
  "result_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the result type */,
  "damping_id" INTEGER /* ID of the damping level */,
  "ia_value" REAL /* Intensity measure (IA) value */,
  "date_calculated" TEXT /* Date the result was calculated */,
  "result_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the result */,
  "result_quality" TEXT /* Quality rating of the result */,
  "result_reliability" TEXT /* Reliability rating of the result */,
  "result_methodology" TEXT /* Methodology used to calculate the result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_results_damping_id FOREIGN KEY ("damping_id") REFERENCES damping_levels ("damping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_results_result_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_type_id") REFERENCES result_types ("result_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_results_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES seismic_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, researcher, admin) */,
  "user_department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "user_position" TEXT /* Position or title of the user */,
  "user_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "user_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the seismic event being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the event */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the event was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used to access the data (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "access_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES seismic_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the seismic event */,
  "methodology" TEXT /* Methodology used for the analysis */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date the analysis was performed */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or documentation */,
  "metadata_quality" TEXT /* Quality rating of the metadata */,
  "metadata_reliability" TEXT /* Reliability rating of the metadata */,
  "metadata_version" TEXT /* Version number of the metadata */,
  "metadata_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the metadata */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_metadata_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES seismic_events ("event_id")
)
